name: The Emily and the Caroline
summary: An early Supreme Court case establishing that statutes should not be read in ways that defeat their own purpose when a reasonable alternative construction is available — a foundational principle of statutory interpretation.
significance: General Prelogar cited this early case in rebuttal for the principle that courts need not interpret a statute in a self-defeating manner when a plausible alternative construction exists, arguing respondents' reading would let any manufacturer evade the Gun Control Act through a trivial workaround.
